mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or has been cut over the prior month, discount rates ease faster than earnings expectations, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. This signal is confirmed if rate declines precede positive forward equity returns and rejected if cuts are followed by flat or negative returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines do not coincide with above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign of the return response is negative in more than 60% of easing episodes, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above recent trend forces tighter policy expectations and lower equity valuations, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed. The claim fails if inflation surprises above trend without a contemporaneous negative equity response.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-acceleration episodes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average 20d return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE with simultaneous PAYEMS deterioration indicates labor market weakening that should depress cyclical earnings expectations and weigh on the S&P 500 over the next 60 trading days. The signal is testable by checking whether labor deterioration episodes systematically precede negative forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if labor-deterioration triggers are not followed by negative 60d SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if adding PAYEMS does not improve out-of-sample directional accuracy versus UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if stronger sales do not translate into higher equity returns after the release window.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth episodes are followed by negative or zero 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average forward return is not statistically above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply while GDPC1 growth is already slowing, the housing-led demand slowdown signals weaker future activity and lower equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The claim is falsified if housing weakness does not add predictive content beyond GDP growth alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing drawdowns accompanied by slowing GDP do not produce negative 60d SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the interaction term is not significant out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO after a prior contraction signals improving industrial supply conditions and better profit prospects, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is confirmed only when industrial production turns up from a weak base.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month after at least 2 prior monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO rebound episodes are not followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than 50%, or if the median return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE regime with stable PAYEMS supports risk appetite because investors infer durable domestic demand and limited recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This is a regime-conditioned claim that should work only when unemployment is already near cycle lows.
failure_condition: Reject if, in low-UNRATE regimes, SPX 20d returns are not positive on average or if adding PAYEMS does not materially improve hit rate relative to UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL inflation and a high FEDFUNDS level squeezes real policy conditions and raises recession risk, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism breaks if high rates are offset by disinflation and equities still advance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation rises above 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered restrictive-inflation episodes are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average return is not below the unconditional benchmark.
